info.title: maui
info.description: CLI for .NET MAUI development environment setup and device management

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: MAUI Development Tools - Device management and environment setup

options.name: --json
options.description: Output in JSON format for machine consumption [default: False]

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Show what would be done without making changes [default: False]

options.name: --ci
options.description: CI mode - non-interactive, fail on first error [default: False]

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Check system for MAUI development readiness

options.name: --fix
options.description: Attempt to automatically fix issues [default: False]

options.name: --platform
options.description: Check only specific platform (dotnet, android, windows)

options.name: --platform
options.description: Filter by platform (android, apple, windows, all) [default: all]

options.name: --accept-licenses
options.description: Non-interactively accept all SDK licenses [default: False]

options.name: --packages
options.description: SDK packages to install (replaces defaults; comma-separated or multiple --packages flags)

arguments.name: packages
arguments.description: SDK packages to install (prompted interactively if omitted) [default: System.String[]]

options.name: --available
options.description: List packages available for installation [default: False]

options.name: --all
options.description: List both installed and available packages [default: False]

options.name: --package
options.description: System image package (auto-detects most recent if not specified)

options.name: --force
options.description: Overwrite existing emulator with the same name [default: False]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: AVD name to start (prompted interactively if omitted) [default: ]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: AVD name to delete (prompted interactively if omitted) [default: ]

commands.name: devflow
commands.description: Automate MAUI apps via Agent API and Blazor WebViews via CDP

options.name: --agent-port
options.description: Agent HTTP port (auto-discovered via broker, .mauidevflow, or default 9223) [default: 9223]

options.name: --platform
options.description: Target platform (maccatalyst, android, ios, windows) [default: maccatalyst]

options.name: --no-json
options.description: Force human-readable output even when piped [default: False]

commands.name: cdp
commands.description: Blazor WebView automation via Chrome DevTools Protocol

options.name: --webview
options.description: Target WebView by index, AutomationId, or element ID (default: first WebView)

commands.name: querySelectorAll
commands.description: Find all elements by CSS selector

arguments.name: selector
arguments.description: CSS selector of element to click

commands.name: snapshot
commands.description: Get simplified DOM snapshot with element refs

commands.name: source
commands.description: Get page HTML source from a WebView

options.name: --automationId
options.description: Resolve element by AutomationId (instead of element ID)

options.name: --type
options.description: Resolve element by type (used with --automationId or alone)

options.name: --index
options.description: Index when multiple elements match (0-based, default: first) [default: 0]

options.name: --and-screenshot
options.description: Take screenshot after action (optional: output path)

options.name: --and-tree
options.description: Dump visual tree after action [default: False]

arguments.name: elementId
arguments.description: Element ID to tap (optional if --automationId, --type, or --text is used)

options.description: Resolve element by AutomationId (instead of element ID)

options.name: --type

options.name: --index
options.description: Index when multiple elements match (0-based, default: first) [default: 0]

options.name: --and-screenshot
options.description: Take screenshot after action (optional: output path)

options.name: --and-tree
options.description: Dump visual tree after action [default: False]

arguments.name: elementId
arguments.description: Element ID (optional if --automationId, --type, or --text is used)

options.name: --automationId
options.description: Resolve element by AutomationId (instead of element ID)

options.name: --type
options.description: Resolve element by type (used with --automationId or alone)

options.name: --index
options.description: Index when multiple elements match (0-based, default: first) [default: 0]

options.name: --and-screenshot
options.description: Take screenshot after action (optional: output path)

options.name: --and-tree
options.description: Dump visual tree after action [default: False]

arguments.name: elementId
arguments.description: Element ID to clear (optional if --automationId, --type, or --text is used)

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Overwrite existing file (default: fail if exists) [default: False]

options.name: --max-width
options.description: Resize screenshot to this max width (overrides auto-scaling)

options.name: --scale
options.description: Scale mode: 'native' keeps full HiDPI resolution, default auto-scales to 1x logical pixels

commands.name: status
commands.description: Check if a recording is in progress

commands.name: scroll
commands.description: Scroll content by delta, item index, or scroll element into view

options.name: --element
options.description: Element ID to scroll into view or to scroll within

options.name: --item-index
options.description: Item index to scroll to (for CollectionView/ListView)

options.name: --position
options.description: Scroll position: MakeVisible (default), Start, Center, End

options.name: --automationId
options.description: Resolve element by AutomationId (instead of element ID)

options.name: --type
options.description: Resolve element by type (used with --automationId or alone)

options.name: --index
options.description: Index when multiple elements match (0-based, default: first) [default: 0]

arguments.name: elementId
arguments.description: Element ID to focus (optional if --automationId, --type, or --text is used)

commands.name: detect
commands.description: Check if an alert/dialog is visible

options.name: --udid
options.description: Simulator UDID (auto-detects booted simulator if omitted)

options.name: --pid
options.description: Mac Catalyst app PID (auto-detects if omitted)

options.name: --udid
options.description: Simulator UDID (auto-detects booted simulator if omitted)

options.name: --pid
options.description: Mac Catalyst app PID (auto-detects if omitted)

arguments.name: button
arguments.description: Button label to tap (default: first accept-style button)

options.name: --udid
options.description: Simulator UDID (auto-detects booted simulator if omitted)

options.name: --pid
options.description: Mac Catalyst app PID (auto-detects if omitted)

commands.name: grant
commands.description: Grant a permission (no dialog will appear)

options.name: --udid
options.description: Simulator UDID (auto-detects booted simulator if omitted)

arguments.name: service
arguments.description: Permission service (camera, location, photos, contacts, microphone, calendar, all, etc.)

options.name: --udid
options.description: Simulator UDID (auto-detects booted simulator if omitted)

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset permission (app will be prompted again)

options.name: --udid
options.description: Simulator UDID (auto-detects booted simulator if omitted)

options.name: --limit
options.description: Number of log entries to return [default: 100]

options.name: --skip
options.description: Number of newest entries to skip [default: 0]

options.name: --source
options.description: Filter by log source: native, webview, or all (default: all)

options.name: --follow
options.description: Stream logs in real-time (Ctrl+C to stop) [default: False]

options.name: --replay
options.description: Number of recent entries to replay on connect (use with --follow, 0 to skip) [default: 100]

options.name: --limit
options.description: Maximum number of entries to show [default: 100]

options.name: --limit
options.description: Maximum number of entries to show [default: 100]

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: Request ID to show details for

commands.name: platform
commands.description: Query platform features and device info

commands.name: app-info
commands.description: Get app name, version, package name, theme

commands.name: device-info
commands.description: Get device manufacturer, model, OS version

commands.name: battery
commands.description: Get battery level, state, power source

commands.name: connectivity
commands.description: Get network access and connection profiles

commands.name: version-tracking
commands.description: Get version history and first launch info

arguments.name: permission
arguments.description: Permission name (e.g., camera, locationWhenInUse). Omit to check all.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List available sensors and their status

arguments.name: sensor
arguments.description: Sensor name (accelerometer, barometer, compass, gyroscope, magnetometer, orientation)

options.name: --duration
options.description: Duration in seconds (0 = indefinite, Ctrl+C to stop) [default: 0]

options.name: --throttle
options.description: Minimum ms between readings (default 100 = ~10/sec, 0 = no throttle) [default: 100]

commands.name: update-skill
commands.description: Download the latest maui-ai-debugging skill from GitHub

options.name: --branch
options.description: GitHub branch to download from [default: main]

commands.name: skill-version
commands.description: Check the installed skill version and compare with remote

options.name: --branch
options.description: GitHub branch to check against [default: main]

commands.name: diagnose
commands.description: Check DevFlow health: broker, agents, and project integration

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Wait for an agent to connect to the broker

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Maximum seconds to wait for an agent to connect [default: 120]

options.name: --project
options.description: Filter by project path (csproj). Resolves to full path for matching.

options.name: --wait-platform
options.description: Filter by platform (e.g., macOS, iOS, Android)

commands.name: batch
commands.description: Execute commands from stdin with JSONL responses on stdout

options.name: --continue-on-error
options.description: Continue executing after a command fails [default: False]

options.name: --human
options.description: Human-readable output instead of JSONL [default: False]

commands.name: commands
commands.description: List all available commands (machine-readable schema discovery)

commands.name: mcp
commands.description: Start MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI agent integration via stdio
